free_text: Precise resonance parameter measurement in the
12C(p,a)13N astrophysically important reaction
Solid state targets were prepared using electron beam
evaporation of natural carbon onto 0.5 mm thick
tantalum backings. Two carbon targets were used with
thicknesses of 1.8+-0.1 and 2.4+-0.1 keV at Ep=1747 keV
determined by the yield curve analysis of the
Ep=1747 keV resonance of 13C(p,g)14N, correspond to a
surface density of carbon atoms of
respectively.
Targets were irradiated in a Faraday-type target
chamber with proton beam of 0.5 - 4.0 uA. Three HPGE
detector were placed around the targetat 0, 90 and 112
degrees. Angular distribution was studied using two
detectors at at 55 and 90 degree positions.
Dead time was kept below 2%.
Reaction yield curves in the vicinity of the Ep=460 and
1700 keV resonances were measured with energy steps of
The experimental yields measured at E projectile energy
were fitted with a single Breit-Wigner formula.
A simplified R-matrix analysis was also performed to
extract resonance energies and widths.
The energy of the primary beam vas determined to a
precision of better than 1 keV.

target thickness     (5%)
peak area            (5%)
detector efficiency (12%)
